column1: What is AWS CodePipeline used for?
column2: A continuous delivery service for fast and reliable application updates that models and visualizes the software release process and automates build, test, and release when code changes
A secure, highly scalable, managed source control service that hosts private Git repositories
Automates code deployments to any instance and handles the complexity of updating applications

column1: What is AWS CodeBuild used for?
column2: Compiles source code, runs tests, and produces ready-to-deploy software packages

column1: What infrastructure details does Elastic Beanstalk handle as a platform-as-a-service?
column2: Capacity provisioning, load balancing, and monitoring

column1: Does Elastic Beanstalk create infrastructure like EC2 instances and load balancers?
column2: Yes, Beanstalk stands up infrastructure via CloudFormation

column1: What programming languages does Elastic Beanstalk support?
column2: Java, .NET, PHP, Node.js, Python, Ruby, and Go (NOT Objective-C)
Application code and the architecture to support an environment for the application to run

column1: What is the fastest way to achieve spin-up time for new EC2 instances?
column2: Bake dependencies into the AMI (though package updates should be performed frequently)

column1: What can Elastic Beanstalk deploy to?
column2: EC2 instances, SQS queues, and ECS tasks

column1: What AWS services can Elastic Beanstalk access?
column2: S3 buckets and RDS databases (cannot install GuardDuty agents or manage WorkSpaces)

column1: How do you control access to Elastic Beanstalk resources?
column2: Use IAM policies to control access to resources attached to users, groups, and roles

column1: What two types of roles does Elastic Beanstalk create?
column2: Service role (to access AWS services) and instance role (to access instances)

column1: Does Elastic Beanstalk have additional charges beyond deployed services?
column2: No, Elastic Beanstalk runs at no additional charge; you only pay for services deployed

column1: What is required for horizontal scaling on EC2?
column2: Both an Application Load Balancer and an Auto Scaling group (Auto Scaling group alone is not sufficient)
